# ddff_environment (Resource)

Manages a Datadog feature flag environment.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ddff_environment" "production" {
  name    = "Production"
  queries = ["env:production"]

  is_production                 = true
  require_feature_flag_approval = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name shown in the Datadog UI.
- `queries` (List of String) Datadog tag queries that define which traffic this environment captures. At least one entry is required.

### Optional

- `is_production` (Boolean) Marks the environment as production. Defaults to `false`.
- `require_feature_flag_approval` (Boolean) Requires a reviewer to approve every feature flag change in this environment. Defaults to `false`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `description` (String) Description shown alongside the environment in the Datadog UI. Read-only via this API.
- `id` (String) UUID assigned by Datadog.
- `key` (String) Stable, human-readable key for the environment, derived by Datadog from the name.
- `updated_at` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Import an existing Datadog feature flag environment by its UUID.
terraform import ddff_environment.production 4f8e31e1-307f-4a70-a245-9b2426d25415
```
